memoryArtifacts.headline: Biographical Wikipedia Stub
memoryArtifacts.description:
Richard de Vernon, Lord of Nether Haddon, was an English noble.

Life
Richard was the son of Richard de Vernon and Margaret de Vipont.[1] He died in 1329.

Marriage and issue
He married, firstly, Alia …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Vernon於大約1255年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾時，他的父親Richard de Vernon20歲，母親Margaret de Vieuxpont22歲。他於1292年與Juliana de Vesci在英國德比夏爾哈登 霍爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1329年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾哈盧亞斯頓享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Vernon于大约1255年出生在英国英格兰德比夏爾时，他的父亲Richard de Vernon20岁，母亲Margaret de Vieuxpont22岁。他于1292年与Juliana de Vesci在英国德比夏爾哈登 霍爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1329年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾哈盧亞斯頓享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vernon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Vernon in Eure, France, named from the Gaulish element ver(n) ‘alder’ + the Gallo-Roman locative suffix -o (genitive -ōnis).
French: habitational name from the same place as in 1 above or from any of several other places in France with the same name and etymology.

variants.name: Varnon
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Vernon  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Vernon was born about 1255, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard de Vernon, was 20 and his mother, Margaret de Vieuxpont, was 22. He married Juliana de Vesci in 1292, in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died about 1329, in Harlaston, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Vernon est né environ 1255, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Vernon, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Vieuxpont, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Juliana de Vesci en 1292, à Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1329, à Harlaston, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Vernon um 1255 in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Vernon 20 und seine Mutter Margaret de Vieuxpont 22. Er hat 1292 in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England Juliana de Vesci geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1329 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Harlaston, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

Lui sposato Juliana de Vesci nel 1292, nel Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Vernonが約1255年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard de Vernonは20歳，母Margaret de Vieuxpontは22歳であった。1292年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアハッドオン ハルでJuliana de Vesciと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1329年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアハーラストンで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Vernon(이)가 대략 1255년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Vernon(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Vieuxpont(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1292년 영국 더비실르 하던 홀에서 Juliana de Vesci(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1329년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 하를라스턴에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Vernon nasceu aproximadamente 1255, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard de Vernon, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Vieuxpont, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Juliana de Vesci em 1292, em Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1329, em Harlaston, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Vernon родился около 1255 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard de Vernon было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Vieuxpont, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Juliana de Vesci в 1292 г. в местности хаддон холл, дирбишир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1329 г. в местности харластон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет.

Se casó con Juliana de Vesci en 1292, en Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1329, en Harlaston, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 76 años.
